issue_title: Bug: Changing tab with keyboard doesn't update address bar
issue_body:
### Description

When you use a keyboard shortcut to change to the previous or next tab (Ctrl + Shift + Tab or Ctrl + Tab), sometimes it will change the folder contents but not the toolbar and address bar, other times it won't change at all...

In the gif, check for example that the second time changing from home to Music, it will keep the widgets button instead the folder buttons, and the address bar says "Nueva pestaña" instead my Music path.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Open several tabs with differents folder.
2. Start to change tabs with the keyboard shortcut (Ctrl + Shift + Tab or Ctrl + Tab)
3. Check the address bar and see that sometimes it shows an incorrect address.

NOTE: For some reason apening settings and closing it seems to fix the issue until you open a new tab. 

### Expected behavior

It should show the proper address and toolbar.

### Files Version

V 2.0.12.0

### Windows Version

OS Version: Windows 10 21H1 10.0.19043.1202

### Relevant Assets

![GIF 13-09-2021 15-55-22](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11770760/133096806-91d2dcfa-c58d-468f-a8fd-9d5cae2a5f12.gif)

Log files:
[debug_fulltrust.log](https://github.com/files-community/Files/files/7154784/debug_fulltrust.log)
[debug.log](https://github.com/files-community/Files/files/7154785/debug.log)
